icons.market: <rect x="4" y="10" width="3" height="8" rx="0.6"/><rect x="10.5" y="5" width="3" height="13" rx="0.6"/><rect x="17" y="8" width="3" height="10" rx="0.6"/>

events.title: Genesis Block Mined
events.desc: Satoshi Nakamoto mined Bitcoin's first block, earning a 50 BTC reward and launching the first proof-of-work public blockchain.

events.title: Bitcoin Pizza Day
events.desc: A programmer paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas, marking the first real-world purchase using Bitcoin.

events.title: First Mt. Gox Hack
events.desc: The world's largest Bitcoin exchange suffered its first security breach, foreshadowing a much larger collapse to come.

events.title: First Bitcoin Halving
events.desc: Block reward halved from 50 to 25 BTC, marking Bitcoin's first significant supply reduction.

events.title: China Regulates Bitcoin
events.desc: Five Chinese regulatory bodies declared that Bitcoin is not a currency and barred financial institutions from handling it.

events.title: Mt. Gox Collapse
events.desc: Approximately 850,000 BTC — 7% of all Bitcoin at the time — went missing, triggering the industry's first major crisis of confidence.

events.title: Ethereum Mainnet Launch
events.desc: Ethereum's smart-contract platform went live, ushering in programmable blockchain and laying the foundation for DeFi and NFTs.

events.title: ERC-20 Token Standard Proposed
events.desc: Ethereum developers formally proposed the ERC-20 standard, unifying the token interface and laying the groundwork for ICOs and DeFi.

events.title: The DAO Hack & Ethereum Hard Fork
events.desc: An attacker drained ~3.6M ETH via a smart-contract flaw. The subsequent hard fork split Ethereum into ETH and ETC.

events.title: Second Bitcoin Halving
events.desc: Block reward halved from 25 to 12.5 BTC, further slowing the rate of new supply.

events.title: China Bans ICOs
events.desc: Seven government agencies outlawed token fundraising; Chinese exchanges soon shut down or moved offshore.

events.title: Bitcoin All-Time High (2017)
events.desc: Driven by the ICO frenzy and retail speculation, Bitcoin approached $20K before entering a year-long bear market.

events.title: Crypto Winter Begins
events.desc: After the bubble burst, total crypto market cap lost over 80% in a year, ushering in a prolonged downturn.

events.title: Black Thursday: COVID Crash
events.desc: COVID-19 triggered global panic; Bitcoin plunged nearly 50% in a single day, with Ethereum dropping to ~$90.

events.title: Third Bitcoin Halving
events.desc: Block reward halved from 12.5 to 6.25 BTC, setting the stage for the next bull run.

events.title: MicroStrategy Adopts Bitcoin Treasury
events.desc: The first public company to buy Bitcoin in bulk as a treasury reserve, setting a precedent later followed by many others.

events.title: DeFi Summer
events.desc: Liquidity mining ignited a boom; TVL on decentralized exchanges and lending protocols surged tenfold in months.

events.title: Beeple NFT Sells for $69M
events.desc: Christie's held its first crypto-denominated auction; Beeple's digital artwork sold for $69M, catapulting NFTs into the mainstream.

events.title: Coinbase Lists on Nasdaq
events.desc: The first major US crypto exchange to go public, reaching an $86B valuation on its debut.

events.title: El Salvador Makes Bitcoin Legal Tender
events.desc: The first sovereign nation to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender, aiming to use it for payments and remittances.

events.title: London Hard Fork / EIP-1559
events.desc: EIP-1559 introduced a base fee burning mechanism, reforming Ethereum's fee market and adding deflationary pressure to ETH.

events.title: China Bans All Crypto Trading & Mining
events.desc: Ten agencies jointly declared all crypto-related activities in China to be illegal financial operations.

events.title: Bitcoin All-Time High (2021)
events.desc: Bitcoin peaked at ~$69K, fueled by institutional capital and leveraged trading.

events.title: Wormhole Bridge Hack
events.desc: An attacker exploited a cross-chain bridge vulnerability between Ethereum and Solana, stealing ~$320M.

events.title: Ronin Network Hack by Lazarus Group
events.desc: Axie Infinity's sidechain validators were compromised; ~$625M stolen, later attributed to North Korea's Lazarus Group.

events.title: Terra / LUNA Collapse
events.desc: UST de-pegged and entered a death spiral, wiping out tens of billions in days — a systemic turning point for the industry.

events.title: Ethereum The Merge
events.desc: Ethereum transitioned from proof-of-work to proof-of-stake, cutting energy consumption by ~99.9% in one of crypto's most significant upgrades.

events.title: FTX Files for Bankruptcy
events.desc: The world's second-largest exchange collapsed after misusing customer funds; its founder was later convicted of fraud.

events.title: Ordinals Protocol Launches on Bitcoin
events.desc: Built on the Taproot upgrade, Ordinals brought NFT-like inscriptions to Bitcoin, sparking the BRC-20 and inscription ecosystem.

events.title: SVB Collapse & USDC De-peg
events.desc: Circle's $3.3B in reserves were trapped in the failed bank; USDC dropped to $0.87 before federal intervention restored the peg.

events.title: SEC Sues Binance & Coinbase
events.desc: The SEC sued both exchanges in the same month, alleging they operated unregistered securities trading platforms.

events.title: CZ Pleads Guilty; Binance Pays $4.3B
events.desc: Binance's founder pleaded guilty to AML violations, stepped down as CEO, and the company reached a record settlement with the DOJ.

events.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs Approved
events.desc: BlackRock, Fidelity, and others received approval to list spot Bitcoin ETFs in the US, opening a compliant channel for traditional capital.

events.title: Fourth Bitcoin Halving
events.desc: Block reward halved from 6.25 to 3.125 BTC, further slowing the rate of new supply issuance.

events.title: DMM Bitcoin Hack
events.desc: A Japanese exchange lost ~$308M, later attributed to North Korea's Lazarus-affiliated group.

events.title: Spot Ethereum ETFs Launch
events.desc: Following Bitcoin, spot Ethereum ETFs received approval and began trading on US exchanges.

events.title: Bybit: Largest Crypto Heist in History
events.desc: North Korea's Lazarus Group manipulated a multi-sig wallet interface to steal ~$1.46B from cold storage — the largest single heist in crypto history.

events.title: US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve
events.desc: The US integrated ~200K BTC seized by the DoJ into a national reserve with a no-sale commitment, setting a sovereign precedent.

events.title: Circle IPOs on NYSE
events.desc: USDC issuer went public on the NYSE, marking the stablecoin industry's arrival in mainstream capital markets.

events.title: GENIUS Act Signed into Law
events.desc: The first US federal stablecoin regulatory framework took effect, requiring payment stablecoins to be fully backed by high-liquidity assets.

events.title: Hong Kong Stablecoin Licensing
events.desc: Hong Kong's stablecoin ordinance took effect, requiring issuers operating in the territory to obtain a license.

events.title: SEC & CFTC Jointly Clear Spot Crypto Products
events.desc: A joint statement confirmed that current US law does not prohibit national exchanges from listing spot Bitcoin and Ethereum products.

events.title: Crypto Black Tuesday
events.desc: Missed macro estimates and cascading liquidations triggered over $19B in forced closures in a single day; Bitcoin dropped >13% in 24 hours.

events.title: Digital Asset Market Clarity Act Advances
events.desc: The Senate Banking Committee passed a procedural vote 15-9, paving the way for federal crypto market structure legislation.
